source_pr.title: *: support collect and async load stats for mv index
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?


Issue Number: ref #46539


### What is changed and how it works?

1. For analyzing mv index, use an independent v2 analyze index task to handle it.
    1. When building analyze tasks, add a new `AnalyzeIndexTask` for each mv index. (`planner/core/planbuilder.go`)
    2. When storing the analyze result, specially handle mv index, like the row count, etc. See the comments for details. (`executor/analyze_idx.go`, `statistics/handle/handle.go`, `statistics/histogram.go`, `statistics/analyze.go`)
3. For loading stats, in `initStats()`, we consider all columns instead of pruned columns to make stats for the virtual columns (and therefore expression index, including mv index) able to be async loaded. (`planner/core/stats.go`)
4. For estimation on mv index, we keep it using pseudo estimation to make its behavior unchanged. We'll enable the estimation after it's supported. (`planner/cardinality/row_count_index.go`)

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select id, table_schema, table_name, partition_name, job_info, processed_rows, state from mysql.analyze_jobs order by id"). (executor/test/analyzetest/analyze_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select id, table_schema, table_name, partition_name, job_info, processed_rows, state from mysql.analyze_jobs order by id"). (executor/test/analyzetest/analyze_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select id, table_schema, table_name, partition_name, job_info, processed_rows, state from mysql.analyze_jobs order by id").
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select id, table_schema, table_name, partition_name, job_info, processed_rows, state from mysql.analyze_jobs order by id"). (executor/test/analyzetest/analyze_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select id, table_schema, table_name, partition_name, job_info, processed_rows, state from mysql.analyze_jobs order by id"). (executor/test/analyzetest/analyze_test.go)
